title: [BUG] Invalid string length
author: Coande
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

<!-- A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->

Lib version: 4.3.0

## Steps To Reproduce

<!-- The exact steps required to reproduce the issue, ideally with a code example -->
I have a large xlsx file export by third part system, has 153MB.

Use:

```
const workbook = new Excel.Workbook();
await workbook.xlsx.readFile(file);
const worksheet = workbook.worksheets[0];
const row = worksheet.getRow(1);
console.log(row.values);
```

Error:

```
(node:4584) UnhandledPromiseRejectionWarning: RangeError: Invalid string length
    at Array.join (<anonymous>)
    at concat (C:\Users\Coande\Desktop\excel-to-db\node_modules\exceljs\node_modules\jszip\lib\stream\StreamHelper.js:51:30)
    at StreamHelper.<anonymous> (C:\Users\Coande\Desktop\excel-to-db\node_modules\exceljs\node_modules\jszip\lib\stream\StreamHelper.js:96:61)
    at Immediate.<anonymous> (C:\Users\Coande\Desktop\excel-to-db\node_modules\exceljs\node_modules\jszip\lib\utils.js:381:18)
    at processImmediate (internal/timers.js:464:21)
(Use `node --trace-warnings ...` to show where the warning was created)
(node:4584) UnhandledPromiseRejectionWarning: Unhandled promise rejection. This error originated either by throwing inside of an async function without a catch block, or by rejecting a promise which was not handled with .catch(). To terminate the node process on unhandled promise rejection, use the CLI flag `--unhandled-rejections=strict` (see https://nodejs.org/api/cli.html#cli_unhandled_rejections_mode). (rejection id: 1)
(node:4584) [DEP0018] DeprecationWarning: Unhandled promise rejections are deprecated. In the future, promise rejections that are not handled will terminate the Node.js process with a non-zero exit code.
```

## The expected behaviour:

<!-- A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen. -->
Read file normally.

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

I don't know.

comments.author: asherklein
comments.body: Hi @Coande ! I got the same issue, how did you solve it?

comments.author: Coande
comments.body:
Not solve it yet. Also with this issue https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/1864 , I replace it to [xlsx-extract](https://github.com/ffalt/xlsx-extract)

> Hi @Coande ! I got the same issue, how did you solve it?

comments.author: kipertech
comments.body: I encountered this bug yesterday, and I believe that having the large Excel file caused this. My Excel file was having 1 million rows, I split it into 2 files and it worked perfectly again.

comments.author: YuzeLou
maoxian-1
